publishedAbstract: - Trying to redeploy a new OSD (in this example / whole document we assume osd-1 has the issue), the osd pod is in pending: ~~~ rook-ceph-osd-1-5dfc7f94dc-6bnzp 0/2 Pending 0 2m15s ~~~- There is no osd.1 present in ceph layer (`ceph osd df` and `ceph osd dump`)- osd-1 deployment exist in openshift, but there is no `rook-ceph-osd-prepare` pod created for this osd- From the bastion try to delete the osd (so after deletion rook will trigger the creation of a new osd) ~~~ # oc process-n openshift-storage ocs-osd-removal-p FORCE_OSD_REMOVAL=true-p FAILED_OSD_IDS=1 | oc create-f- ~~~ but the ocs-osd-removal-job pod fails with the error ~~~ # oc logs-n openshift-storage job ocs-osd-removal-job-xxxx [client.admin] keyring = /var/lib/rook/openshift-storage/client.admin.keyring 2024-02-27 12:56:38.658774 D | exec: Running command: ceph osd dump--connect-timeout=15--cluster=openshift-storage--conf=/var/lib/rook/openshift-storage/openshift-storage.config--name=client.admin--keyring=/var/lib/rook/openshift-storage/client.admin.keyring--format json 2024-02-27 12:56:38.965991 I | cephosd: validating status of osd.1 2024-02-27 12:56:38.966192 C | multus-validation: failed to get osd status for osd 1: not found osd.1 in OSDDump ~~~
publishedTitle: ODF ocs-osd-removal-job pod failed : not found osd.X in OSDDump
